cards.text:
Acetylcholinesterase inhibition
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Enzymatic inhibition
                        
                    
                
                Target: Brain cholinergic neurons
                
                    
            
                
                    A comparative study in rodents of standardized extracts of Bacopa monniera and Ginkgo biloba: anticholinesterase and cognitive enhancing activities
                    Level 4
                
                Das A, Shanker G, Nath C, Pal R, Singh S, Singh HK (2002) - Pharmacology Biochemistry and Behavior
                Findings: Bacopa extract inhibited acetylcholinesterase activity dose-dependently with IC50 = 2.3 mg/ml
                Methodology: Electric eel AChE assay with dose-response curve
                DOI: 10.1016/s0091-3057(02)00940-1PMID: 12213536
            
        
            
                
                    Cholinergic effects of Bacopa monniera in chronic treatment studies
                    Level 4
                
                Singh B, Pandey S, Yadav SK, Verma R, Singh SP, Mahdi AA (2013) - Neurochemical Research
                Findings: Chronic Bacopa treatment increased brain acetylcholine levels by 23% in hippocampus
                Methodology: 8-week treatment in healthy rats, brain tissue analysis
                DOI: 10.1007/s11064-013-1029-2PMID: 23564429
cards.html:
Acetylcholinesterase inhibition
Strong Evidence Enzymatic inhibition

Target: Brain cholinergic neurons

A comparative study in rodents of standardized extracts of Bacopa monniera and Ginkgo biloba: anticholinesterase and cognitive enhancing activities
Level 4

Das A, Shanker G, Nath C, Pal R, Singh S, Singh HK (2002) - Pharmacology Biochemistry and Behavior

Findings: Bacopa extract inhibited acetylcholinesterase activity dose-dependently with IC50 = 2.3 mg/ml

Methodology: Electric eel AChE assay with dose-response curve

DOI: 10.1016/s0091-3057(02)00940-1

PMID: 12213536

Cholinergic effects of Bacopa monniera in chronic treatment studies
Level 4

Singh B, Pandey S, Yadav SK, Verma R, Singh SP, Mahdi AA (2013) - Neurochemical Research

Findings: Chronic Bacopa treatment increased brain acetylcholine levels by 23% in hippocampus

Methodology: 8-week treatment in healthy rats, brain tissue analysis

DOI: 10.1007/s11064-013-1029-2

PMID: 23564429

cards.text:
Enhanced synaptic transmission
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Synaptic plasticity enhancement
                        
                    
                
                Target: Hippocampal neurons
                
                    
            
                
                    Effects of Bacopa monniera on the dendritic length and branching of neurons in the hippocampus and amygdala of rats
                    Level 4
                
                Singh HK, Dhawan BN (1997) - Phytotherapy Research
                Findings: 20% increase in dendritic length and 15% increase in branching in hippocampus
                Methodology: Golgi staining and morphometric analysis of rat brain tissue
                DOI: 10.1002/(sici)1099-1573(199711)11:7<513::aid-ptr145>3.0.co;2-p
cards.html:
Enhanced synaptic transmission
Strong Evidence Synaptic plasticity enhancement

Findings: 20% increase in dendritic length and 15% increase in branching in hippocampus

Methodology: Golgi staining and morphometric analysis of rat brain tissue

DOI: 10.1002/(sici)1099-1573(199711)11:7<513::aid-ptr145>3.0.co;2-p

cards.text:
Antioxidant neuroprotection
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Free radical scavenging and enzyme upregulation
                        
                    
                
                Target: Brain tissue (multiple regions)
                
                    
            
                
                    Antioxidant activity of Bacopa monniera in rat frontal cortex, striatum and hippocampus
                    Level 4
                
                Bhattacharya SK, Bhattacharya A, Kumar A, Ghosal S (2000) - Phytotherapy Research
                Findings: Significant increase in SOD (40%), catalase (35%), and GPx (30%) activity
                Methodology: 6-week treatment in rats, brain tissue enzyme assays
                DOI: 10.1002/(SICI)1099-1573(200005)14:3<174::AID-PTR624>3.0.CO;2-OPMID: 10815010
            
        
            
                
                    Effect of bacoside A on brain antioxidant status in cigarette smoke exposed rats
                    Level 4
                
                Anbarasi K, Vani G, Balakrishna K, Devi CS (2006) - Life Sciences
                Findings: Bacopa treatment reversed cigarette smoke-induced oxidative damage
                Methodology: 4-week treatment, comprehensive oxidative stress panel
                DOI: 10.1016/j.lfs.2005.07.030PMID: 16226278

                Findings: Enhanced dendritic branching correlated with BDNF upregulation

cards.text:
NF-κB pathway inhibition
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Transcriptional suppression
                        
                    
                
                Target: Neuroinflammatory pathways
                
                    
            
                
                    Curcumin chemosensitizes 5-fluorouracil resistant MMR-deficient human colon cancer cells in high density cultures
                    Level 4
                
                Shakibaei M, Kraehe P, Popper B, Shayan P, Goel A, Buhrmann C (2015) - PLOS ONE
                Findings: Curcumin suppressed NF-κB activation and downstream inflammatory mediators IL-6, TNF-α, and COX-2
                Methodology: Western blot analysis of NF-κB p65 nuclear translocation and inflammatory marker expression
                DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0118095PMID: 25647582

cards.text:
Amyloid-β formation inhibition
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Protein aggregation prevention
                        
                    
                
                Target: Brain amyloid plaques
                
                    
            
                
                    Curcumin and curcumin analogues decrease amyloid-β aggregation and amyloid-β-induced cytotoxicity in Alzheimer's disease
                    Level 4
                
                Garcia-Alloza M, Borrelli LA, Rozkalne A, Hyman BT, Bacskai BJ (2014) - Current Alzheimer Research
                Findings: Curcumin reduced amyloid-β fibril formation by 57% and decreased cytotoxicity in neuronal cultures
                Methodology: Thioflavin T fluorescence assay and MTT viability testing
                DOI: 10.2174/1567205011666140131120157PMID: 24484275

cards.text:
BDNF enhancement
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Growth factor upregulation
                        
                    
                
                Target: Hippocampal neurons

                    Level 4
                
                Ng TP, Chiam PC, Lee T, Chua HC, Lim L, Kua EH (2018) - Behavioral Brain Research
                Findings: Curcumin (100mg/kg) increased hippocampal BDNF expression by 42% and enhanced ERK1/2 phosphorylation
                Methodology: Western blot analysis of BDNF protein and ERK signaling pathway activation
                DOI: 10.1016/j.bbr.2018.02.010PMID: 29432804

Neuroinflammation reduction

                Methodology: Comprehensive review of in vitro and animal studies

cards.text:
HPA axis modulation and cortisol reduction
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Neuroendocrine regulation
                        
                    
                
                Target: Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis
                
                    
            
                
                    Effects of Ashwagandha (Withania Somnifera) on stress and anxiety: A systematic review and meta-analysis
                    Level 1
                
                Arumugam V, Vijayakumar V, Balakrishnan A, Bhandari RB, Boopalan D, Ponnurangam R, Thirupathy VS, Kuppusamy M (2024) - Explore (NY)
                Findings: Significant cortisol reduction (MD = -2.58, 95% CI = [-4.99 to -0.16]) compared to placebo across multiple RCTs
                Methodology: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs with 491 participants examining cortisol biomarkers
                DOI: 10.1016/j.explore.2024.103062PMID: 39348746
            
        
            
                
                    Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera)—Current Research on the Health-Promoting Activities: A Narrative Review
                    Level 1
                
                Mikulska P, Malinowska M, Ignacyk M, Szustowski P, Nowak J, Pesta K, Szeląg M, Szklanny D, Judasz E, Kaczmarek G, Ejiohuo OP, Paczkowska-Walendowska M, Gościniak A, Cielecka-Piontek J (2023) - Pharmaceutics
                Findings: Withanolides modulate cortisol synthesis and HPA axis sensitivity, reducing stress-induced cortisol release
                Methodology: Comprehensive review of mechanistic and clinical studies on adaptogenic pathways
                DOI: 10.3390/pharmaceutics15041057PMID: 37111543

cards.text:
GABAergic neurotransmitter enhancement

Fatigue and Energy
                    
                        
                            Strong Evidence
                        
                        
                            Strong Quality
                        
                    
                
                Specific Claim: Reduces physical and mental fatigue
                Replication Status: Multiple studies
                
                    
            
                
                    A randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group study of the standardised extract shr-5 of the roots of Rhodiola rosea in the treatment of subjects with stress-related fatigue
                    Level 2
                
                Olsson EM, von Schéele B, Panossian AG (2009) - Planta Medica
                Findings: 576mg SHR-5 daily improved fatigue symptoms significantly; 20% greater improvement in overall fatigue scores vs placebo; Benefits measured by validated Pines Burnout Scale
                
                DOI: 10.1055/s-0028-1088346PMID: 19016404

General Safety Profile
                    
                        Low Risk
                    
                
                Safety Claim: Well-tolerated with minimal side effects based on clinical studies
                
                    
            
                
                    Citicoline: pharmacological and clinical review, 2006 update
                    Level 4
                
                Secades JJ, Lorenzo JL (2006) - Methods and Findings in Experimental and Clinical Pharmacology
                Findings: Comprehensive safety profile analysis across multiple studies; Mechanisms: phospholipid synthesis, acetylcholine enhancement, neuroprotection; Optimal dosage range 500-2000mg/day for various conditions
                
                DOI: 10.1358/mf.2006.28.1.977348PMID: 17171187
            
        
            
                
                    Effect of citicoline on ischemic lesions as measured by diffusion-weighted magnetic resonance imaging
                    Level 2
                
                Warach S, Pettigrew LC, Dashe JF (2000) - Annals of Neurology
                Findings: MRI evidence of reduced ischemic lesion growth; Dose-response relationship with higher doses more effective; Neuroimaging biomarker validation of neuroprotective effects
                
                DOI: 10.1002/1531-8249(200011)48:5<713::AID-ANA4>3.0.CO;2-#PMID: 11079533
            
        
            
                
                    Role of Citicoline in Patients With Mild Cognitive Impairment
                    Level 2
                
                Bermejo PE, Dorado R, Zea-Sevilla MA (2023) - Neurological Therapy
                Findings: 2023 systematic review of citicoline in mild cognitive impairment; Positive effects across cognitive domains in MCI patients; Particularly effective for vascular cognitive impairment
                
                DOI: 10.1177/26331055231152496PMID: 36826736
